title: Psychotic Mom Thinks She Can Get Away With Double Murder

beats.description: Opens with a dramatic interrogation snippet where Drake is told his grandmother is dead, immediately grabbing attention.
Backtracks to the initial 911 call reporting Marilyn missing, followed by the welfare check and interviews with her brother and neighbor.
Officers visit the rental property, interview Alyssa, and uncover family tensions, eviction history, and suspicious details like wall holes and neck scratches.
A charred body is discovered in a remote area, later confirmed via dental records to be Marilyn, shifting the case from missing person to homicide.
Detectives inform Marilyn's brother, explore the history of Ryan's death, and secure search warrants while piecing together the family's volatile dynamics.
Execution of the search warrant reveals blood evidence, leading to the arrests of Alyssa, Drake, and Annie. Intense interrogations follow, culminating in conflicting confessions.
Annie provides the full, detailed confession, DNA evidence confirms the crime scene, and the video concludes with the trio's plea deals and sentencing.

_analysis_metadata.transcript_preview: Hello. Are you dressed? >> Yeah. >> Hi, Detective Wattis. Hello. >> How are you? Good. >> Detectives are making their way inside 19-year-old Drake Bickicket's apartment. In just a few seconds, he's about to hear something that will change his life forever. >> So, your grandma, we found her, but she's dead. Drake's grandmother, 65-year-old Marilyn Gandert, was last seen walking outside her home before disappearing without a trace. Police initially believed they were dealing with a routine missing person's report, but over the next few weeks, this case will slowly turn into one of the most tragic and disturbing double homicide investigations they've ...
